commands.review_summary:
The PR exports `resolveIdentityAvatarUrl`, uses it from `agent.identity.get`, tightens local avatar extension gating, and updates Control UI Personal-card avatar URL selection with focused tests.

PR surface: Source +16, Tests +98. Total +114 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main can return `/avatar/<agentId>` from `agent.identity.get` for workspace-relative avatars while the Control UI avatar route requires read authorization; no live browser session was run in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] A narrow code blocker remains, but missing contributor real behavior proof and maintainer acceptance of the resolver compatibility change make this human-reviewed rather than an automated repair lane.

target.title: fix: render workspace avatars in identity card
target.body:
﻿Closes #97602

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where users configuring a workspace-relative agent avatar would see a broken avatar in the Control UI Personal card because `agent.identity.get` returned an auth-gated `/avatar/<agentId>` URL that a plain image element cannot load with Bearer auth.

## Why This Change Was Made

The identity RPC now reuses the existing workspace-bounded avatar data URI projection before falling back to the Control UI avatar route. The shared projection preserves the local avatar image-extension policy so unsupported workspace files are not inlined as avatar data.

The Control UI Personal card now prefers the renderable identity data URL when the backend has already resolved one, while still avoiding stale `/avatar/<agentId>` route previews when avatar resolution rejected the source.

AI-assisted with Codex.

## User Impact

Control UI can render valid workspace-relative Personal card avatars consistently with the agent list/chat surfaces, without making an unauthenticated `/avatar/<agentId>` image request. Unsupported local avatar files remain blocked and reported through the existing avatar status/reason fields.

## Evidence

- Red/green backend regression: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts -- -t "returns workspace-relative avatars as data URLs"`
- Red/green backend security regression: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts -- -t "does not inline unsupported workspace avatar files"`
- Red/green UI route regression: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/app-render.assistant-avatar.test.ts -- -t "keeps identity data URLs renderable"`
- Red/green UI rejected-source regression: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/app-render.assistant-avatar.test.ts -- -t "does not render stale route avatars"`
- Focused identity coverage: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts -- -t "agent.identity.get"`
- Avatar resolver coverage: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts -- -t "avatar"`
- UI render coverage: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/views/config-quick.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-render.assistant-avatar.test.ts`
- `git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD`

Notes:
- Full `src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts` on this Windows checkout still has unrelated pre-existing failures around SQLite temp cleanup `EBUSY` and `/tmp` path normalization, so focused avatar coverage was used for this PR.
- Local autoreview was rerun after the fixes, but the Codex review engine hit the account usage limit before returning a structured result.
